summary: The primary source for the sensory innervation of the cornea comes from branches of the first division of the trigeminal nerve. This mainly involves the long and short ciliary nerves with significant contribution coming through the nasociliary branch. To directly address your query about where it gets its sensation primarily, focus would be placed upon these specific neural pathways originating within or near the orbit region supplying direct connections into ocular tissues including but not limited to just surface layers like those found across much of eye’s anterior segment such as conjunctiva etc., yet most notably being responsible specifically towards deeper parts especially central areas due their close proximity thereupon making them crucially important clinically speaking when considering pain responses among other sensations experienced during various conditions affecting eyesight health maintenance procedures amongst others involving surgical interventions impacting visual apparatus integrity functionality preservation enhancement purposes alike thus ensuring proper management strategies can effectively target affected regions based accurate understanding underlying mechanisms involved therein facilitating optimal patient care outcomes overall thereby enhancing quality life experiences associated therewith accordingly emphasizing importance role played hereunder regarding precise anatomical
